text.title: django-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for Django test design beyond the basic pytest setup.

rule.statement: Test runner is `pytest + pytest-django`. Configure via `pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options]` with `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "config.settings.test"`.
rule.rationale: pytest-django integrates db, settings, and fixtures cleanly. Pinning settings module makes test runs deterministic.

rule.statement: Test data created via `model_bakery.baker.make` (terse) or `factory_boy` factory (explicit). NO `Model.objects.create()` inline.
rule.rationale: Centralised data creation; field changes don't cascade across every test file.

rule.statement: Service-layer tests invoke the real service function, the real ORM, and the real utils. Mocks ONLY at process boundaries (HTTP, S3, third-party SDKs, email backend).
rule.rationale: Mocking internal services tests the mocks. Real-code service tests catch real bugs; in-memory DB + boundary mocks keep them fast.

rule.statement: DRF endpoint tests use `rest_framework.test.APIClient` + `force_authenticate(user=user)`. JSON requests via `client.post(url, payload, format="json")`.
rule.rationale: APIClient handles auth, serialization, and DRF-specific request lifecycle. Plain `Client` produces wrong content negotiation and broken auth in DRF endpoints.

rule.statement: DB-touching tests use `@pytest.mark.django_db` (transactional) by default. Use `@pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)` only when the test needs to commit (e.g., signal testing, raw SQL).
rule.rationale: Default transactional isolation rolls back at test end → fast. `transaction=True` flushes the DB → slow.
